label: May 2026
summary: 4 Active Conditions identified: Type 2 Diabetes, Diabetic Dyslipidemia, Hypertension, and Fungal Skin Infection. Consultation with a specialist is recommended soon.

sections.title: Diabetes Markers
sections.note: HbA1c of 12.7% reflects average blood glucose over the past 90 days. At this level, consistent management is needed to prevent long-term complications in the kidneys, eyes, and heart.

sections.title: Lipid Profile
sections.note: Elevated triglycerides and a high TC/HDL ratio indicate metabolic stress. Improving these markers will significantly reduce cardiovascular risk.

sections.title: Kidney Function
sections.note: The kidneys are currently working at a high capacity (hyperfiltration). A Urine Microalbumin test is recommended to check for early signs of stress.

sections.title: How Everything Connects
items: Severely Uncontrolled Diabetes (3+ months) — The root of all current conditions.
High Triglycerides → Causes pancreatic stress and impacts digestion.
High LDL + Low HDL → Increases future risk of arterial buildup.
Hypertension → Multiplies pressure on kidneys and heart.
Fungal Infection → High blood sugar in skin creates a feed-source for fungus.
sections.note: Controlling the root cause (blood sugar) will naturally improve all the connected conditions listed above.

consultationPlan.note: Monitor Blood Pressure and initiate protective measures.
